summary.name: Value-Quality Dividend FCF Reversal Longs
summary.author: Jing Wu
summary.description: The strategy is a long only US equity factor portfolio that seeks to capture a combined value and quality premium by selecting stocks with strong shareholder payout, strong balance sheet value, and attractive free cash flow valuation, while favoring recent underperformers consistent with a short term reversal effect. summary.description_full:
The strategy is a long only US equity factor portfolio that seeks to capture a combined value and quality premium by selecting stocks with strong shareholder payout, strong balance sheet value, and attractive free cash flow valuation, while favoring recent underperformers consistent with a short term reversal effect. Each month at the start of the month, the universe is formed from US stocks with available fundamentals, first restricting to the 300 most liquid names by dollar trading volume to enhance implementability. The strategy then ranks candidates on four signals: higher trailing three month dividends per share, lower 1 month price change (contrarian tilt), higher book value per share, and higher free cash flow yield, and combines the ranks into a composite score to select 10 names.

Positions are established and held with an equal weight allocation across the selected names, with rebalancing scheduled monthly. The alpha assumption is structurally bullish for the selected basket with a 30 day expected holding horizon, so the portfolio maintains continuous long exposure to the top ranked names and refreshes the holdings at each monthly selection. Risk controls are primarily via liquidity screening, diversification across 10 stocks, equal weighting, and a fixed starting capital base; there are no explicit drawdown, stop loss, leverage, or volatility targeting overlays. Decisions are made on a monthly cadence using fundamental data plus 1 month price performance.

raw.name: Value-Quality Dividend FCF Reversal Longs
